title: Ectopic beat-compensated electrical heterogeneity information
abstract: The exemplary systems and methods may be configured for use in the determination of ectopic beat-compensated electrical heterogeneity information. Electrical activity can be monitored by a plurality of external electrodes. Ectopic beat information can be detected. Ectopic beat-compensated electrical heterogeneity information can be generated based on the monitored electrical activity and the detected ectopic beat information.
claims: Claims 1. A system comprising: electrode apparatus comprising a plurality of external electrodes to monitor electrical activity from tissue of a patient; and computing apparatus comprising processing circuitry and coupled to the electrode apparatus and configured to: monitor electrical activity using the plurality of external electrodes; provide ectopic beat information; and generate ectopic beat-compensated electrical heterogeneity information based on the monitored electrical activity and the ectopic beat information . 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the plurality of external electrodes comprises surface electrodes positioned in an array configured to be located proximate skin of a torso of the patient. 3. The system of any of claims 1-2, wherein the system further comprises a display, wherein the display comprises a graphical user interface configured to assist a user in evaluating patient cardiac health, wherein the computing apparatus is further configured to display the ectopic beat-compensated electrical heterogeneity information on tire graphical user interface. 4. The system of any of claims 1-3, wherein the generating the ectopic beat- compensated electrical heterogeneity information comprises generating the ectopic beat- compensated electrical heterogeneity information based on electrical activity monitored during at least one ectopic beat 5. The system of any of claims 1-4, wherein the generating the ectopic beat- compensated electrical heterogeneity information comprises generating ectopic beat- compensated electrical heterogeneity information based on electrical activity monitored during at least one non-ectopic beat. 6. The system of claim 5, wherein the at least one non-ectopic beat comprises one of a beat monitored while delivering cardiac pacing therapy and an intrinsic beat monitored in the absence of delivering cardiac pacing therapy. 7. The system of any of claims 1-6, wherein the ectopic beat information comprises a percentage of ectopic beats of a plurality ' of beats over a selected period of time. 8. The system of any of claims 1-7, wherein the ectopic beat information is provided using at least one of an implantable cardiac device, a subcutaneous cardiac monitor, an external cardiac monitor, and the plurality of external electrodes of the electrode apparatus. 9. The system of any of claims 1-8, comprising a cardiac therapy apparatus to deliver cardiac therapy based on the generated ectopic beat-compensated electrical heterogeneity information, wherein the computing apparatus is further configured to modify the cardiac therapy in response to the generated ectopic beat-compensated electrical heterogeneity information 10. The system of claim 9, wherein the modification of the delivered cardiac therapy is repeated until the generated ectopic beat-compensated electrical heterogeneity information is less than or greater than a threshold. 11. The system of claim 9, wherein: the modification of the delivered cardiac therapy compri ses modifying the delivered cardiac therapy based on a pre-determined set of settings, and a determination is made as to which setting of the pre-determined set of settings results in minimal ectopic-beat compensated electrical heterogeneity' information.
